description: The Georgian mafia is an organized crime network originating in Georgia, known for producing the largest number of 'thieves in law' in post-Soviet states. It controls and regulates many Russian-speaking mafia groups and is active in Russia and Europe. The organization has two major clans from Tbilisi and Kutaisi, with the Kutaisi Clan being particularly well represented. Its criminal activities include arms trafficking, extortion, money laundering, and murder.

country_links.evidence_quote: Georgian gangsters fled the country, typically to Russia, Israel and Western Europe.
country_links.context: Georgian gangsters fled to Israel to avoid prosecution in Georgia, indicating a presence there.

country_links.evidence_quote: The activities of the Georgian mafia in Italy are concentrated mainly in the city of Bari...
country_links.context: The Georgian mafia is active in Italy, concentrated in Bari, involved in burglaries, money laundering, and trafficking of weapons and uranium.

country_links.evidence_quote: controls and regulates most of the Russian-speaking and fully controls Russia and Georgia mafia groups.
country_links.context: The Georgian mafia controls and regulates most Russian-speaking mafia groups and is very active in Russia, with a presence in Moscow and involvement in the criminal underworld.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Georgian mafia operates in Spain through criminal groups involved mainly in violent crimes and organized property offenses...
country_links.context: The Georgian mafia operates in Spain through criminal groups involved in violent crimes, burglaries, and money laundering, with police operations in Barcelona and Madrid.
